_meta.purpose: Maps v2 cluster page slugs (clusteros.io/clusters/<slug>) to the v3 data scope that should populate their Sankey + Headlines. v3 owns the canonical mapping; v2's generate-clusters.js reads this at build time. New v2 cluster pages need a new entry here.

schema.v2_display_name: Human-readable name from the v2 page
schema.supercluster: v2 supercluster slug (/superclusters/<slug>) — every cluster page is a leaf of a supercluster tree. Used for navigation AND for the null-fallback Sankey when the cluster has no v3 mapping
schema.v3_region: Region slug matching data/sankey/_index.json keys, or null
schema.v3_cluster: Cluster slug (life_sciences|space|energy|digital_creative|advanced_manufacturing|fintech), '_all' for the region-overview Sankey, or null when no v3 data backs this page
schema.subcluster_filter: Optional: subcluster slug to narrow the Sankey further. Only valid when v3_cluster is a thematic cluster, not '_all'. Requires generate_projections.py to have run for the region; today only 5 of 25 regions have projections.
schema._notes: Editorial commentary on the mapping decision — read these first when red-lining

_meta.slug_pattern: v2 cluster slug = '<supercluster-slug>-<cluster-display-name-hyphenated>'. CamelCase display names split on case boundaries: CleanEnergy → clean-energy, AdvancedManufacturing → advanced-manufacturing, HealthTech → health-tech, AgriFood → agri-food, FinTech → fin-tech, LifeSciences → life-sciences. Single-word display names stay as-is (Fintech → fintech, Chemicals → chemicals).

behaviours.v3_cluster = '<slug>': Embed Sankey filtered to (region, cluster) — the cream SVG capture renders this view
behaviours.v3_cluster = '<slug>' + subcluster_filte: Embed Sankey filtered to (region, cluster, subcluster). Falls through to parent (region, cluster) view if the subcluster doesn't exist in v3's projections.
behaviours.v3_cluster = '_all': Embed the region-overview Sankey (all clusters, no filter) — Innovation Ecosystem pages, region-overview BY DESIGN not by fallback
behaviours.v3_cluster = null: Prose-only page; no Sankey. Composite block renders a banner: 'No cluster-specific Sankey yet for this domain — see the supercluster reading for the broader region →' pointing at /superclusters/<supercluster>. Chosen over a region-overview Sankey fallback because the latter would render multiple null pages with the SAME Sankey (Hull has 4 null entries; identical Sankeys with disclaimers reads worse than honest 'not yet'). Forward-compatible with the planned build_sankey_data.py extension that honours per-region cluster declarations — when that ships, most null entries become mappable and only the genuinely-thin v2-only domains (likely Logistics, possibly Chemicals if classifier yield is too thin) stay prose-only.

priority_1_quantum_critical_tech.rationale: Likely the same dual-use / sensitive-tech classifier project — quantum overlaps materially with what 'critical technologies' tends to designate (quantum, AI, semiconductors, advanced materials, biotech of strategic concern). Building once covers two ecosystems and probably surfaces equivalents in Cambridge, Manchester, Edinburgh.

priority_2_agrifood.rationale: Hull's AgriFood is one of several — Lincolnshire, Devon, Cardiff, Norfolk likely surface the same ecosystem on v2. Worth a classifier seed when 3+ regions need it. UKRI grant register has a reasonable surface area here (BBSRC, food-system research programmes).

priority_3_chemicals.rationale: Hull (Saltend/BP), Tees Valley, Cheshire (Runcorn/Widnes), Grangemouth — recurring across UK regions. EPSRC chemistry grants + Innovate UK industrial decarbonisation gives a defensible classifier surface.

priority_4_logistics.rationale: Genuinely thin in UKRI's grant register — logistics R&D is mostly private. Classifier ROI low. Park.

projection_coverage_gap.regions_pending: the remaining 20 in configs/sankey/ — generate_projections.py needs to run against them once the run_overnight.ps1 UKRI-outcomes + CH refresh completes. Until then, any subcluster_filter entry in this registry falls through to the parent (region, cluster) view.

_meta.review_status: DRAFT v3 — null entries switched to prose-only treatment (option (a)) after deciding the supercluster-Sankey-with-disclaimer fallback would render multiple null pages with identical Sankeys (Hull's 4 nulls would all repeat the same chart). Forward-compatible with the planned build_sankey_data.py extension to honour per-region cluster declarations (memo at docs/MEMO_build_sankey_data_extension.md). Once that extension ships, most current nulls become mappable. Registry not yet propagated to the remaining 22 regions — awaiting decision on whether to extend v3 or live with registry-as-aliaser.

greater-glasgow-critical-technologies._notes: Classifier seed roadmap priority 1 (Quantum + Critical Technologies). Until v3 gains the classifier, page is prose-only with a 'see the supercluster reading →' banner linking to /superclusters/greater-glasgow.

greater-glasgow-innovation-ecosystem._notes: Region-overview meta-cluster. Sankey shows the no-filter view: Funders → Insts → all 6 v3 clusters → Outcomes. Highest evidence count on v2's supercluster page (264) — this is the most-viewed Sankey of the region.

greater-glasgow-net-zero._notes: Naming alias: v2 'Net Zero' = v3 'energy'. Worth aligning eventually — either v3 renames its cluster to 'net_zero' (semantically more accurate; UKRI's framing has shifted that way) or v2 renames to 'energy'. Until then: alias here.

greater-glasgow-fintech._notes: Glasgow uses 'Fintech' (single capital) — slug is 'fintech', not 'fin-tech'. West of England uses 'FinTech' (camel) — slug there is 'fin-tech'. v2 display-name inconsistency, but the rule (split on case boundaries) handles both.

west-of-england-creative._notes: PAIRED SPLIT — West of England has two v2 clusters (Creative + Digital) both mapping to v3 digital_creative. The intended split (Creative = arts/media/Aardman/broadcast; Digital = AI/software/data) requires a subcluster_filter. v3 has not generated subcluster projections for West of England yet (only 5 of 25 regions projected today), so both pages currently fall back to the parent cluster Sankey — meaning they render the SAME Sankey. This is the honest state until generate_projections.py runs against WoE. Editorial gap to be aware of; not a slug issue.

west-of-england-fin-tech._notes: Slug uses 'fin-tech' (camelCase split) because v2 displays 'FinTech', unlike Glasgow's 'Fintech' which slugs to 'fintech'.

west-of-england-aerospace._notes: Bristol/Airbus/Rolls-Royce/GKN make aerospace a region-defining strength — genuinely distinct from generic adv_manufacturing. Glasgow has a precision_aerospace subcluster within advanced_manufacturing, but West of England has no projections yet. Falls back to parent cluster Sankey until generate_projections.py runs against WoE. When that happens, add subcluster_filter: 'precision_aerospace'.

west-of-england-digital._notes: PAIRED SPLIT — see west-of-england-creative. Same Sankey until WoE subcluster projections land.

west-of-england-quantum._notes: Bristol's quantum ecosystem is real (Quantum Engineering Tech Labs, Bristol Quantum Information Institute). Classifier seed roadmap priority 1 (paired with Glasgow Critical Technologies). Page is prose-only with a 'see the supercluster reading →' banner linking to /superclusters/west-of-england until classifier lands.

west-of-england-life-sciences._notes: v2 display 'LifeSciences' (camel, no space) → slug 'life-sciences' (split on case boundary).

hull-east-yorkshire-chemicals._notes: Saltend / BP / Humber petrochemicals — region-defining for Hull. Classifier seed roadmap priority 3. Page is prose-only with a 'see the supercluster reading →' banner linking to /superclusters/hull-east-yorkshire until classifier lands.

hull-east-yorkshire-advanced-manufacturi.v2_display_name: Hull and East Yorkshire Advanced Manufacturing

hull-east-yorkshire-logistics._notes: Hull's port + Humber freight cluster. Classifier seed roadmap priority 4 (deprioritised — logistics R&D footprint is thin in UKRI's grant register). Page is prose-only with a 'see the supercluster reading →' banner, likely indefinitely — even if classifier work runs, grant yield may be too thin for a meaningful Sankey.

hull-east-yorkshire-clean-energy.v2_display_name: Hull and East Yorkshire Clean Energy
hull-east-yorkshire-clean-energy._notes: Naming alias: 'CleanEnergy' = 'energy'. Humber has a strong offshore-wind + CCUS story — might warrant subcluster split eventually if v3's energy projections grow finer for Hull.

hull-east-yorkshire-digital._notes: v2 'Digital' (singular, no paired Creative split like West of England) → maps to v3 digital_creative whole. Confirmed alias not null.

hull-east-yorkshire-health-tech.v2_display_name: Hull and East Yorkshire Health Tech
hull-east-yorkshire-health-tech._notes: Naming alias: 'HealthTech' = v3 'life_sciences'. Note v3's life_sciences is broader (covers basic research + therapeutics + medical devices + digital health); the v2 page's prose can keep its medtech framing while consuming the parent Sankey.

hull-east-yorkshire-agri-food._notes: Humber agrifood (Cranswick, Greencore, NPIF agritech). Classifier seed roadmap priority 2. Page is prose-only with a 'see the supercluster reading →' banner linking to /superclusters/hull-east-yorkshire until classifier lands.

hull-east-yorkshire-innovation-ecosystem.v2_display_name: Hull and East Yorkshire Innovation Ecosystem
hull-east-yorkshire-innovation-ecosystem._notes: Display name uses 'Hull and East Yorkshire' (with 'and') for this entry only — other Hull clusters use 'HullEastYorkshire'. Slug drops 'and' to match the supercluster URL pattern (/superclusters/hull-east-yorkshire). Confirm slug against actual v2 routing.
